news.headline: Crows hold off Cats in thriller for stunning win
news.body: The Crows have overcome the Cats in an enthralling clash.
news.ai_summary: Jordan Dawson (Adelaide) was best afield with 22 disposals and 3 goals in a captain's knock, making him a hold/POD target despite the modest possession count. Young gun Dan Curtin impressed with workrate and a game-saving mark, while recruit Callum Ah Chee tagged Tom Stewart to just 15 disposals and 3 intercepts before Stewart left with a hamstring injury — a major concern for Stewart owners. Adelaide also lost Jordon Butts and Ah Chee to injury, while Taylor Walker may face MRO scrutiny for a shove on Connor O'Sullivan. Ben Keays and debutant Hugo Hall-Kahan (head clash with Toby Murray) are also worth monitoring.

news.headline: Live: Crows stunned by Cats comeback as dangerous push lands Walker in strife
news.body: Geelong claws out of a 20-point deficit in the third quarter as Taylor Walker finds himself in hot water over a mid-air push. Follow live.
news.ai_summary: Taylor Walker (Adelaide Crows) could face MRO scrutiny over a dangerous push, putting his availability at risk for fantasy coaches holding him. Skipper Jordan Dawson (Adelaide) is having a monster night with a goal, 13+ touches, four intercepts and four score involvements, maintaining his perfect 6.0 set-shot record this season. Patrick Dangerfield, Bailey Smith and Max Holmes (Geelong Cats) lead all-comers with 18 disposals apiece at the main break, while Dan Curtin (16 touches, 3 clearances) tops Adelaide's ball-winners. Tom Stewart (Geelong) is also under MRO threat for a hit on Cal Ah Chee, and Jeremy Cameron owners will be sweating after just 5 disposals to half-time.

news.headline: Coach defends doctors as Crows players play out game after head clash
news.body: Matthew Nicks defends his medical staff after two Crows were cleared to play out their win against the Cats without a concussion test after a nasty head clash.
news.ai_summary: Adelaide's Callum Ah Chee (hamstring – his fourth since joining the Crows; 11 disposals, 1 goal) and Jordon Butts (calf) both failed to finish the one-point win over Geelong, with the extent unknown – owners should brace for outs. Debutant Hugo Hall-Kahan and Toby Murray passed concussion checks after a head clash and returned to play. Taylor Walker (2 goals on return) faces MRO scrutiny for shoving Connor O'Sullivan, risking a suspension. Skipper Jordan Dawson was the standout with 22 disposals and 3 goals despite a sore hip, while Patrick Dangerfield posted a season-high 30 disposals and 7 clearances in the loss.

news.headline: Crows, Cats lock in final teams for huge clash. LIVE from 7pm ACST
news.body: Follow all the action from Thursday night's clash between Adelaide and Geelong.
news.ai_summary: Geelong have recalled Jack Bowes, Jack Martin and Ollie Wiltshire for the Thursday night clash with Adelaide, with Lawson Humphries (ankle) out, Mitch Edwards managed and Brad Close dropped — denting the job security of Cats-owned mid-pricers. Adelaide bring back Taylor Walker and hand a debut to mid-season recruit Hugo Hall-Kahan, while Izak Rankine (calf) and Riley Thilthorpe (suspension) are notable fantasy outs. Rankine owners face a hit to scoring with no late changes available, while Hall-Kahan looms as a cheap cash-cow option for those chasing rookie value.

news.headline: Eagles growing confident their darkest days are behind them
news.body: Liam Duggan is confident the Eagles have turned a corner.
news.ai_summary: Selection alert: Brandon Starcevich will debut for West Coast against Port Adelaide on Saturday night, adding a two-time Brisbane premiership defender to fantasy defensive options. Captain Liam Duggan continues to anchor the backline as the Eagles improve to 4-8, with young guns Harley Reid, Reuben Ginbey, Willem Duursma, Cooper Duff-Tytler and Bo Allan all relevant fantasy watches amid improving team form. With winnable matches against Port Adelaide and North Melbourne in Perth before the bye, scoring opportunities should rise for West Coast mids and defenders, potentially trimming break-evens across the board.

news.headline: Hayden Young: Young set to return but Dockers to play it safe with other key players
news.body: Young set to return but Dockers to play it safe with other key players.
news.ai_summary: Fremantle's Hayden Young returns from concussion against North Melbourne, while Matthew Johnson is cleared after a hamstring scare — both back in for fantasy sides. Caleb Serong misses a third game with a calf, and Brennan Cox (knee) may be rested with next week's bye, opening a likely recall for Oscar McDonald. Sean Darcy and Jaeger O'Meara will spend another week in the WAFL, delaying any DPP/ruck owner returns. North Melbourne tagger Finn O'Sullivan is out for two weeks with concussion, removing his tagging threat.

news.headline: Young set to return but Dockers to play it safe with other key players
news.body: Justin Longmuir will take a conservative path with several important players coming back from injury.
news.ai_summary: Hayden Young (Fremantle) returns from concussion this week vs North Melbourne, and Matthew Johnson is cleared after a hamstring scare — both back into fantasy sides post-bye consideration. However, Caleb Serong misses a third straight game with a calf, while Brennan Cox (knee) may be rested with the bye looming, opening a recall for Oscar McDonald. Sean Darcy and Jaeger O'Meara will spend another week in the WAFL, so hold off on ruck/mid trades targeting them. North Melbourne tagger Finn O'Sullivan is out for two weeks with concussion, freeing up Fremantle's mid scoring.

news.headline: Lions go back to basics in bid to break out of slump ahead of QClash
news.body: Chris Fagan has told Brisbane's leaders to worry about their own game first as they seek to lead the premiers back to winning form.
news.ai_summary: Brisbane co-captains Hugh McCluggage and Josh Dunkley remain well below their fantasy ceilings as the Lions chase a turnaround in Saturday's QClash against Gold Coast, with coach Chris Fagan urging his leaders to refocus on their own games. Key defender Harris Andrews also had a rare quiet outing on Fremantle's Josh Treacy, while Cam Rayner, Will Ashcroft, Jaspa Fletcher and Jarrod Berry have all been down on output. Oscar Allen remains sidelined through injury. With Fagan preaching "simplicity" and more freedom, owners of McCluggage, Dunkley and Ashcroft will be hoping for a bounce-back as break-evens climb.

news.headline: Josh Worrell: New Crow told to play on instinct, with a familiar face by his side
news.body: New Crow told to play on instinct, with a familiar face by his side.
news.ai_summary: Adelaide mid-season draftee Hugo Hall-Kahan will debut against Geelong on Thursday night as a rebounding half-back, just nine days after being drafted — a rookie-priced option worth monitoring after averaging 27.7 disposals in the VFL. He'll line up alongside close mate Josh Worrell, with Wayne Milera (team-high 26.3 disposals) the established attacking driver from defence and an All-Australian chance. Tall defender Mark Keane returns via the SANFL this weekend from a lower-leg fracture, threatening to squeeze Crows defensive job security once he's recalled.

Mid-season draftee Hugo Hall-Kahan (22yo defender) debuts as a cheap rookie option. Darcy Fogarty backs up after returning from a bulging disc in the past fortnight.

news.headline: Dee weighing up decision, Hawk close to new deal, Dog to sign on
news.body: Dee weighing up decision, Hawk close to new deal, Dog to sign on.
news.ai_summary: Melbourne free agent Tom Sparrow will delay his contract decision until after the Demons' round 16 bye, with the 2021 premiership midfielder likely to be offered a four-year deal after taking on increased midfield duties — a role boost relevant for SuperCoach owners. Fellow Demons Kade Chandler and Bayley Fritsch are also in re-signing talks, suggesting role stability at Melbourne. Hawthorn's Jarman Impey is closing on an extension through 2029 off the back of an All-Australian-calibre half-back campaign, reinforcing his fantasy job security. Western Bulldogs defender Michael Sellwood is set to sign a two-year extension through 2028 after playing every game this season, locking in his backline role.

news.headline: 'Powerful': Daniher family speaks to Demons, Magpies
news.body: Neale Daniher's wife Jan and daughter Bec spoke to the Pies and Demons at the MCG.
news.ai_summary: Melbourne defender Jake Lever is confirmed to return from concussion for Monday's King's Birthday clash against Collingwood, ending his absence and giving Demons owners a lockdown defender back in their lineup. Melbourne forward Brody Mihocek faces his former club Collingwood for the first time, with Magpies defender Isaac Quaynor flagging he'll tag him closely around goal — a potential handbrake on Mihocek's scoring. With Melbourne losing its past two and Collingwood dropping three of its past four, both sides need the four points, making this a key matchup for fantasy-relevant mids and forwards on both lists.

Western Bulldogs defender Michael Sellwood (every game this season) is set for a two-year extension to 2028, locking in his backline role under Luke Beveridge.

news.headline: 'Never felt more settled': Gun midfielder loving life at the Swans
news.body: Chad Warner says he's loving life at the Swans since signing a two-year extension.
news.ai_summary: Sydney midfielder Chad Warner returned to form against Richmond with 3 goals and 2 assists after a quiet fortnight, alongside Isaac Heeney's monster outing (32 disposals, 13 score involvements, 5 goals) — both worth monitoring as buy-back options. Ruck Brodie Grundy returns this week after being rested, restoring his ceiling for SuperCoach owners, while Hayden McLean (4 goals) faces the axe with the talls all firing. Selection headaches loom with Callum Mills (calf), Matt Roberts (groin), Justin McInerney (ankle) and Malcolm Rosas jnr (ankle) all being assessed for Sunday's St Kilda clash — cover may be needed for Mills and Roberts owners.

news.headline: 'What bye?': Mitchell urges Hawks to focus on Bont, Bulldogs ahead of break
news.body: While Hawthorn will start favourite at the MCG, the Western Bulldogs have won three of their last four and will fancy their chances.
news.ai_summary: Selection boost for Hawthorn: Will Day returned successfully last week, while Tom Barrass (hamstring) and Conor Nash (neck spasms) are nearing recalls ahead of Friday night's clash with the Western Bulldogs. The Bulldogs welcome back ruckman Tim English, restoring his ceiling for owners after a four-game skid. Coach Sam Mitchell highlighted Marcus Bontempelli, Ed Richards, Ryley Sanders and Matt Kennedy as key Bulldogs midfield threats, all relevant for Fantasy scoring. Nick Watson also looms as a risk after his disallowed goal highlighted role/rule uncertainty.

news.headline: Docker O'Driscoll says mental health admission 'best thing' he's done
news.body: Fremantle Dockers midfielder Nathan O'Driscoll says he has received "nothing but love" after posting on social media about his struggles with anxiety and how he contemplated taking his own life.
news.ai_summary: Fremantle Dockers midfielder Nathan O'Driscoll is back in the senior side after speaking publicly about his mental health struggles, having played five games this season from 43 career AFL matches. His return to selection is a watch for fantasy coaches considering cheap Dockers mid options, though role and job security remain the key questions given his limited 2025 output. news.headline: Star defender unsure about Origin future, confident of strong Giants finish
news.body: Sam Taylor is confident the Giants can make a charge to finish the season, but whether he would play Origin again remains to be seen.
news.ai_summary: Sam Taylor (GWS Giants) returned to football on Sunday in the 49-point win over Melbourne, 15 weeks after a high-grade hamstring strain suffered in AAMI AFL Origin for Western Australia. He came to the bench multiple times clutching the hamstring but confirmed it was just cramp in the Alice Springs heat, easing fears for fantasy holders. With 11 games left and Taylor backing the Giants to win most, he's a hold as a defensive rock, though monitor his minutes in coming weeks before trusting him as a key defender option.

news.headline: Tom Green: Giants table monster offer to star mid as cashed-up Devils loom
news.body: Giants table monster offer to star mid as cashed-up Devils loom.
news.ai_summary: Tom Green is unavailable for all of 2025 after a February ACL reconstruction, ruling the Greater Western Sydney midfielder out as a fantasy option this season. The Giants have tabled a seven-year, ~$9 million extension through 2034 to fend off Tasmania, signalling his long-term role as a midfield cornerstone and likely future captain. The 2025 best and fairest winner remains a premium keeper target for 2026 once fully rehabbed, with potential discount pricing depending on his recovery timeline.

news.headline: Bombers seek special assistance package from AFL
news.body: Essendon has followed the lead of North Melbourne, Gold Coast and West Coast in asking for an assistance package to accelerate its return to competitiveness.
news.ai_summary: Essendon has opened talks with the AFL for an end-of-season assistance package after winning just one of its past 25 games and sitting last on the ladder, following the precedent set by West Coast, North Melbourne and Gold Coast. For fantasy coaches, this signals a likely influx of draft picks and expanded rookie list spots at Tullamarine, potentially squeezing job security for fringe Bombers while opening rookie-priced opportunities in 2026. Short term, the injury-ravaged list that frustrated sacked coach Brad Scott remains a problem under interim Dean Solomon after the 30-point loss to West Coast, so tread carefully with Essendon-exposed cash cows. No specific player prices, averages or break-evens were provided in the article.

news.headline: Rebuilding Richmond: Tigers president on Yze, injury crisis, Waverley move, eyeing India
news.body: John O'Rourke speaks to Cal Twomey on the big issues at Richmond amid a strategic rebuild at one of the AFL's giants. Status updated: Status NEWS → INJURY TBC.
news.ai_summary: Richmond president John O'Rourke has reaffirmed coach Adem Yze (9 wins from 58 games, contracted to 2028) amid a long-term rebuild, signalling continued pain for Tigers-owned fantasy assets after a 114-point loss to Sydney. The injury crisis to key young Tigers — Sam Lalor, Josh Gibcus, Taj Hotton, Tom Sims, Josh Smillie, Jonty Faull and Harry Armstrong — is wrecking job security and breakeven value, with no quick returns flagged. With O'Rourke conceding a 6-7 year rebuild, coaches should be wary of Richmond mid-pricers and instead target cheap rookies if/when these kids finally debut or return. A Punt Road redevelopment and possible Waverley/Gosch's Paddock relocation adds further disruption heading into 2026-27.

Coaches should note North Melbourne (Alastair Clarkson) plays back-to-back "home" games in WA against Fremantle and West Coast, a venue/travel factor worth weighing for Roos, Dockers and Eagles assets. Melbourne (Steven King) extended its poor Alice Springs record to 4-8 after losing to GWS, while Richmond heads to Hobart's Ninja Stadium from round 14 to face Brisbane — relevant context for matchup-based trades involving Demons, Tigers and Lions players.

news.headline: They couldn't, could they? How Blues could still be a finals contender
news.body: The Wildcard Round has opened up all kinds of possibilities, and a resurgent Carlton could take full advantage.
news.ai_summary: Carlton has won three straight under interim coach Josh Fraser to sit 4-8, with upcoming fixtures against Essendon, West Coast and Richmond offering fantasy coaches reason to back in Blues premiums for scoring runs. Historically 11-12 wins sneaks a top-10 spot, meaning Carlton's run home keeps the likes of Patrick Cripps, Sam Walsh and George Hewett relevant as captaincy and trade-in targets. With only one interstate trip left (Greater Western Sydney in Round 15) and a Round 14 bye to navigate, timing trades around Carlton's bye will be critical.

news.headline: The 'honest conversations' Swans tall has as domination creates selection conundrum
news.body: Hayden McLean has had to be patient this season, but the Swan hopes more chances are ahead after Sydney's four talls dominated.
news.ai_summary: Hayden McLean (Sydney Swans) is a selection risk for fantasy coaches despite booting 4 goals in his 100th game, as coach Dean Cox refuses to commit to the four-tall structure alongside Charlie Curnow (8 goals), Logan McDonald (2) and Joel Amartey (1). McLean's spot was only opened by injuries to small forwards Tom Papley and Malcolm Rosas jnr, making him vulnerable to being dropped back to the VFL once they return. With Cox calling it "the hardest decision" and rotating his key forwards in and out, McLean remains a hold/avoid for fantasy purposes despite his form. Curnow's eight-goal haul reinforces his value as a premium forward option.

news.headline: Good news Blues roll on as young defender gets Rising Star nomination
news.body: Harry Dean is the Telstra AFL Rising Star nominee for round 12.
news.ai_summary: Carlton's Harry Dean has locked in his defensive role with a Round 12 Rising Star nomination, posting 15 disposals, seven intercepts, three marks and four tackles at 80% game time in the Blues' win over Geelong. Across 10 games the father-son key defender is averaging 4.3 intercepts, 4.1 marks and 4.2 spoils, making him a watchable DPP-style rookie for keeper leagues despite limited fantasy ceiling. With upcoming matchups against Essendon, GWS, West Coast and Richmond, his job security looks rock-solid under interim coach Josh Fraser. Fellow Blue Jagga Smith (Round 1 nominee) remains the other young Carlton name to monitor.

news.headline: Tiger banned for wild off-the-ball strike in VFL
news.body: Richmond's Hugo Ralphsmith has been suspended in the VFL.
news.ai_summary: Richmond's Hugo Ralphsmith has been offered a two-match VFL ban for an off-the-ball strike on Sydney's Max Geddes, delaying his AFL return after recovering from an MCL injury. With the Tigers on a bye in Round 13 and resuming against Brisbane in Round 14, Ralphsmith won't be selection-relevant until at least Round 16. Fantasy coaches holding him as a cash-cow rookie should look elsewhere given Richmond's 19-man injury list isn't enough to fast-track his recall.

news.headline: Heartbreak as young Demon suffers another ACL injury
news.body: Andy Moniz-Wakefield is set for another long stint on the sidelines.

news.headline: Matt Rowell: Suns gun ready to embrace another battle with Lions midfielder
news.body: Suns gun ready to embrace another battle with Lions midfielder.
news.ai_summary: Gold Coast midfielder Matt Rowell shapes as a key QClash matchup play, with Brisbane co-captain Josh Dunkley likely tagging him again — Dunkley has held Rowell to 21 disposals or fewer in five straight meetings, denting his ceiling for fantasy coaches. Rowell did manage 23 disposals and a goal in last year's semi-final loss, so he's not a hard fade, but expect a tagged role. Selection-wise, Will Graham (concussion) returns for the Suns, while Jamarra Ugle-Hagan (ankle) and Lachie Weller (hamstring) are in doubt pending Thursday's main training.

news.headline: 'Hard to agree, but I understood': How Hewett bounced back after shock drop
news.body: George Hewett says his omission from the Blues' side was a tough pill to swallow, but he knew what he had to do to get back.
news.ai_summary: George Hewett (Carlton) is back in the senior side after a one-game VFL stint, where he averaged 33.7 disposals, 8.3 clearances and 6 tackles across three reserves matches to force his recall. He's now playing a forward-mid rotation role swapping with Ben Ainsworth, hitting the scoreboard in recent weeks and starting key centre bounces in the win over Geelong. Fantasy coaches should note the reduced pure-midfield time caps his ceiling, but job security looks restored under interim coach Josh Fraser.

news.headline: Ben McKay: McQualter backs young Eagle for All-Australian nod, Bomber to stay in new role
news.body: McQualter backs young Eagle for All-Australian nod, Bomber to stay in new role.
news.ai_summary: West Coast's Reuben Ginbey firms as a keeper/defender option after a career-best 17-disposal, 11-intercept, 7-spoil, 5-intercept-mark game shutting down Nate Caddy, with coach Andrew McQualter pushing him for All-Australian. Essendon's Ben McKay will stay in his new forward/ruck role under caretaker Dean Solomon after kicking his first goal since 2022 and racking up six clearances — relevant for DPP/positional shifts and his scoring ceiling. West Coast's Milan Murdock (3 goals) and Harley Reid also caught the eye as cash-generation/POD watches. Essendon's Archer May (shoulder) was subbed out and is an injury concern.

news.headline: The struggling Saints should keep Jack forward
news.body: The Bulldogs' next gen is almost here, Sydney's forward half pressure is back plus more lessons from round 12.
news.ai_summary: Fantasy owners of Jack Silvagni (St Kilda) should brace for a permanent forward shift after his 3-goal third-quarter spark vs Hawthorn — bad news for his defensive DPP value but a potential scoring floor-lift with Max King still out. Jagga Smith (Carlton) is must-watch after 27 disposals, 6 clearances and 6 inside-50s vs Geelong, shrugging off Oisin Mullin's tag and trending as a Rising Star/keeper. Reuben Ginbey (West Coast) continues his lockdown role on talls (held Nate Caddy to 10 disposals), capping his ceiling — fade for cash generation. Fremantle's Josh Treacy, Jye Amiss and Patrick Voss (78 goals combined) remain elite forward options, while Western Bulldogs depth pieces Will Lewis and Michael Sellwood are emerging cash cows worth monitoring.

news.headline: Eagles cruise past struggling Bombers for fourth win
news.body: The Eagles have proven too good for the Bombers.
news.ai_summary: West Coast's Harley Reid (24 disposals, 9 score involvements) and Tim Kelly (team-high 28 disposals) reinforced their fantasy credentials in the 30-point win, while Willem Duursma (25 disposals, 6 inside 50s, 1 goal) continues to justify rookie selection. Jake Waterman and Milan Murdock each kicked 3 goals, and key defender Reuben Ginbey shut down Nate Caddy in another strong outing. Essendon's injury crisis deepened with Archie May suffering a dislocated shoulder, while Ben McKay was shifted forward as a Caddy sidekick/back-up ruck — a role change worth monitoring. Bombers midfielders Elijah Tsatas (26 disposals, 6 inside 50s) and draftee Sullivan Robey (19 disposals, game-high 9 clearances) are emerging fantasy options.

news.headline: Jagga Smith: Six things we learned: Jagga is edging clear in the Rising Star race
news.body: Six things we learned: Jagga is edging clear in the Rising Star race.
news.ai_summary: Carlton's Jagga Smith is firming as Rising Star favourite after a 27-disposal, six-clearance, six-inside-50 game against Geelong, following 23 score involvements across the prior fortnight — a must-watch rookie for Fantasy coaches. St Kilda's Jack Silvagni booted 3 goals after a half-time forward switch and, with Max King still sidelined, looks set for a permanent attacking role that hurts his DEF status. Fremantle's Josh Treacy, Jye Amiss and Patrick Voss have now combined for 78 goals through 12 rounds, making all three viable forward options. West Coast's Reuben Ginbey continues his shutdown role (held Nate Caddy to 10 disposals), capping his Fantasy ceiling despite strong job security.

news.headline: Eagles, Bombers lock in final teams. Follow LIVE from 5.20pm AWST
news.body: Follow all the action from Sunday's round 12 games.
news.ai_summary: Essendon (1-10) has been gutted by injury with Sam Durham, Matt Guelfi, Mason Redman, Jye Caldwell and skipper Andy McGrath all out via the medical room — a major fantasy blow given Redman and Caldwell's scoring relevance. Nik Cox, Elijah Tsatas, Will Setterfield, Saad El-Hawli and Max Kondogiannis come in, with Tsatas and Setterfield the most playable cash-cow/job-security options. West Coast (3-8) is unchanged after pushing Collingwood, keeping Eagles holds like Harley Reid and Liam Baker in stable roles. Melbourne v GWS at Alice Springs also has no late changes, so premiums including Christian Petracca, Max Gawn and Tom Green are locked.

news.body: In Cal-culations, Callum Twomey looks at the major changes Carlton has made in the past three weeks and more.

news.headline: Hot Giants down Demons in Red Centre domination
news.body: Melbourne's 'home game' record in Alice Springs continues to  go in the wrong direction after a belting from GWS.
news.ai_summary: Injury watch: Stephen Coniglio (GWS) sat out the second half with hamstring awareness, while Harry Petty (Melbourne) battled a foot issue and Sam Taylor (GWS) had cramp in his return game — monitor all three. With Jesse Hogan (hip) and Jake Riccardi (ankle) out, Leek Aleer played a forward-ruck role and dominated Jacob van Rooyen, boosting Aleer's appeal while denting van Rooyen's output. Finn Callaghan (31 disposals, 2 goals) and Clayton Oliver (28 disposals, 7 clearances despite a head-to-head from Jack Steele) remain premium holds, and Lachie Ash's rebounding role continues to support his scoring. Max Gawn's poor ball use and Melbourne's broken mid-forward connection (62 inside 50s for just 9.16) is a concern for Demons-owned forwards.

news.headline: West Coast claims fourth win as Essendon's injury nightmare continues
news.body: West Coast makes light work of Essendon at Perth Stadium, as the Bombers lose another promising youngster to a serious injury.
news.ai_summary: Essendon's injury crisis deepened with Archer May dislocating his shoulder, adding to a 16-man list — bad news for owners as the Bombers slumped to 1-11 under interim coach Dean Solomon. West Coast fantasy relevants fired: Tim Kelly (28 disposals, 554m gained), Harley Reid (24 and a goal), Reuben Ginbey (BOG in defence) and forwards Jake Waterman (3.3, 9 marks) and Milan Murdock (3 goals, 19 disposals) all cashed in. Ben McKay was a shock selection at full-forward but had zero early impact, while Archie Roberts (30 disposals, 570m), Zach Merrett (28 and a goal) and Sullivan Robey (19 and 9 clearances) were the Bombers' main accumulators. Nate Caddy (2 late goals) was held goalless for three quarters by Ginbey, denting his scoring ceiling.

news.headline: Dogs hang on to claim a cliffhanger against charging Pies
news.body: Western Bulldogs led Collingwood comfortably for most of the night before it came down to the wire.
news.ai_summary: Ed Richards was the standout fantasy play for the Western Bulldogs, exploding with 12 disposals and four inside 50s in the final quarter alone, while Marcus Bontempelli (22 disposals, nine clearances, one goal) and Jordan De Goey (30 disposals, one goal, five goal assists) also produced strong ceilings. Tim English returned in the ruck but Rory Lobb continued sharing the role and pushing forward, capping English's scoring upside. Nick Daicos was elite again with 37 disposals, five clearances and a goal for Collingwood. Forward watch: preseason signing Will Lewis booted three goals and Arty Jones (1 goal, 3 assists) impressed, while Lachie Schultz (14 disposals, two goals) remains a low-output option.

news.headline: Fagan keeps the faith in shaky Lions, improvement left in surging Dockers
news.body: An.'angry' Chris Fagan let fly at his team during the three-quarter time break.
news.ai_summary: Brisbane Lions stars are misfiring badly, with co-captains Josh Dunkley (16 disposals), Hugh McCluggage (13) and Cam Rayner (7) all flopping in a 25-point loss to Fremantle — a concern for SuperCoach owners as Chris Fagan vowed to stick with his out-of-form group through a third straight defeat. Lions defender Keidean Coleman is expected to miss 1-2 weeks with a hamstring injury, opening a downgrade/DPP hole. Fremantle's Matthew Johnson (hamstring) and Brennan Cox (knee, no structural damage) both failed to finish the game and shape as injury risks for Round 13. Coaches holding underperforming Lions premiums should weigh a likely bounce-back in next week's QClash against Gold Coast before trading.

news.headline: Tigers grapple with injury crisis as Swans loss exposes gap
news.body: Richmond considered selecting mid-season draftee Kye Annand just days after he was drafted as injury woes continue to bite.
news.ai_summary: Richmond's injury crisis deepens after a 114-point loss to Sydney, with coach Adem Yze revealing he considered debuting mid-season draftee Kye Annand on Charlie Curnow (8 goals), instead handing the job to second-year Luke Trainor. Tom Lynch (larynx) and Jonty Faull (concussion) are expected back post-bye, which should free Noah Balta to return to his preferred role — a fantasy boost for his scoring consistency. On the Sydney side, Justin McInerney (ankle) and Lewis Melican (hamstring) face scans, putting their Round 13 availability against St Kilda in doubt. Isaac Heeney (32 disposals, 13 score involvements, 5 goals) and Curnow loom as must-have premiums in red-hot form.

news.headline: Sliding Lions' pain continues as top Dockers dominate
news.body: Brisbane's stretch of poor form has lengthened after a third consecutive loss, this time to Fremantle.
news.ai_summary: Fremantle thumped Brisbane by 25 points, with the Dockers' tall forward trio dominating: Jye Amiss (4 goals), Patrick Voss (4) and Josh Treacy (3) feasted on a depleted Lions backline. Matthew Johnson (20 disposals, 1 goal) tagged Lachie Neale early before being released, while Shai Bolton (21 and 3 goal assists), Andrew Brayshaw (24) and Jordan Clark (24) were all influential — relevant for Fantasy owners. Injury concerns: Brennan Cox (knee) didn't return after half-time for Fremantle, and Brisbane's Keidean Coleman left in the first quarter with a hamstring, joining Dayne Zorko, Ryan Lester, Lincoln McCarthy, Jack Payne, Oscar Allen and Eric Hipwood on the Lions' casualty list. Darcy Wilmot and Zac Bailey provided run for Brisbane and remain worth monitoring as cash-gen/keeper options.

news.headline: Final teams in as Dogs go toe-to-toe with Magpies. Follow LIVE
news.body: Follow all the action from Saturday's round 12 games.
news.ai_summary: Tim English returns for the Western Bulldogs after two weeks out with concussion, a major fantasy boost given his ruck scoring ceiling, while Luke Cleary (concussion) is omitted. Cody Weightman remains at least a week away from returning from his knee issue. Collingwood lose key defender Darcy Moore (hamstring), Jamie Elliott (knee) and Will Hayes (shoulder), opening up roles for inclusions Wil Parker, Harvey Harrison, Tim Membrey and Angus Anderson. Adelaide coaches should also note Taylor Walker is set to be recalled for the Geelong clash.

news.headline: Swans flex muscles in 114-point belting of Tigers
news.body: Sydney has defeated Richmond by 114 points at the SCG.
news.ai_summary: Charlie Curnow (Sydney) exploded for 8.1 against Richmond, smashing his ceiling and giving SuperCoach owners a huge cash/points boost after being outmuscled early by Luke Trainor. Isaac Heeney was the fantasy standout with 32 disposals, 5 clearances and 5 goals, while Nick Blakey (28 disposals, 409m gained) and fill-in ruck Peter Ladhams (24 disposals, 9 clearances, 28 hitouts, 1 goal — covering rested Brodie Grundy) also feasted. Injury concerns hit Justin McInerney (ankle, subbed out) and Lewis Melican (hamstring), both in doubt for St Kilda, while Callum Mills, Tom Papley and Malcolm Rosas remain sidelined. Hayden McLean celebrated game 100 with 4 goals, boosting his role/job security in the forward mix.

news.headline: League concedes Dempsey point should have been reviewed
news.body: The AFL has confirmed the decision to rule Ollie Dempsey's goal a behind should have been reviewed by the ARC.
news.ai_summary: No fantasy-relevant selection, injury, role or pricing news here — this article concerns an umpiring/ARC controversy in Carlton's 12.12 (84) to Geelong's 12.16 (88) four-point win, where Ollie Dempsey's opening-minute shot was incorrectly ruled a behind. Coaches should note Patrick Cripps and Sam Walsh were credited by Chris Scott as starring in the midfield for Carlton, which is the only on-field takeaway for Fantasy/SuperCoach purposes. Geelong (8-4) next face Adelaide on Thursday night, a short six-day turnaround worth monitoring for any late changes to Cats fantasy assets.

news.headline: Live: Swans complete crushing win over Tigers ahead of key Lions-Dockers clash
news.body: Charlie Curnow finishes with eight goals in Swans's crushing win over Richmond, before the Brisbane Lions face a tough task against the high-flying Fremantle at the Gabba. Follow live.
news.ai_summary: Tim English (Western Bulldogs) returned from concussion to kick 2 goals in the Bulldogs' 4-point win over Collingwood — a key SuperCoach hold given his ruck scarcity. Ed Richards (24 disposals, 2 goals) and Matt Kennedy (29) backed up Marcus Bontempelli (22 disposals, 9 clearances), while first-year forward Will Lewis booted 3 — one to watch as a cheap rookie. Nick Daicos (37 touches, 1 goal) and Jordan De Goey (30 disposals, 19 score involvements, 1 goal) were enormous in defeat and remain premium must-haves. Charlie Curnow's 8-goal haul in Sydney's 114-point thrashing of Richmond should spike his price and break-even — relevant for forward-line upgraders.

news.headline: Tigers talk tough about targeting father-son picks
news.body: New draft rules encourage bids on club-aligned talent, and Richmond plans to make the most of advantages.
news.ai_summary: This is a draft-focused article with minimal immediate fantasy relevance. Richmond recruiting boss Rhy Gieschen has confirmed the Tigers (currently second-last) are willing to bid on Carlton father-son Cody Walker and Port Adelaide Academy prospect Dougie Cochrane to secure a second-round compensation pick. Richmond is also targeting 205cm ruck Harry van Hattum, who could be a long-term Rookie Draft watch for Fantasy ruck stocks. No current player prices, scores, break-evens or injuries are mentioned.

news.headline: Blueprint or blip? What the loss to Geelong told us about the Swans
news.body: Geelong's pressure and midfield dominance in round 11 gave Sydney a reality check.
news.ai_summary: James Jordon (Sydney) shapes as a tagging option after restricting Geelong's Bailey Smith to just 8 disposals in the first half before Dean Cox controversially released the tag — Smith then exploded for 18 disposals, 3 clearances and a goal in the third quarter alone, highlighting Smith's ceiling when unattended. Sydney's midfield was smashed -25 in contested possessions at half-time with a -5 centre clearance differential, a concern for owners of Chad Warner, Isaac Heeney and Brodie Grundy. The Swans' turnover game also collapsed (-32 differential vs a league-best +18.7 average), denting scoring upside for high-priced Swans across the board. Lawson Humphries showed intercept appeal as a cheap defender watch.

news.headline: How Lions' premiership formula is breaking without its foot soldiers
news.body: Every premiership team needs its role players and Brisbane is running in short supply at the moment.
news.ai_summary: Brisbane recalls Noah Answerth (3 games in 2026 after back-to-back concussions) and Jarrod Berry (6 games) for Saturday's clash with Fremantle, a boost for owners after a month out. Their absence, plus Brandon Starcevich's offseason departure to West Coast, has left the Lions leaking goals to small/medium forwards — Toby Greene (5), Shaun Mannagh (5) and Will Hayward (3) in recent weeks — with 14 players used in defence already. Callum Ah Chee remains the key defensive forward stopper, while Darcy Wilmot and Jimmy Tunstill have filled in as run-with options. No fantasy-relevant scoring, price or break-even figures were provided.

news.headline: Bolt from the Blues: Carlton snatches thriller over Cats
news.body: Carlton has defeated Geelong by four points in a rollicking contest at the MCG.
news.ai_summary: Patrick Cripps sealed Carlton's 88-84 thriller over Geelong with a late mark and goal, continuing strong form for SuperCoach owners. Geelong's Lawson Humphries was helped off in the fourth quarter with an awkward landing — monitor his status. Sam Walsh was prolific before being hurt in a Bailey Smith tackle, leaving the ground holding his right shoulder; a key injury watch for owners. Carlton youngsters Jagga Smith, Flynn Young (two goals), Talor Byrne, Jack Ison, Harry Dean and Billy Wilson all impressed in expanded roles under Josh Fraser, while Jeremy Cameron (4 goals) and Ollie Dempsey remain in strong form for the Cats.

news.headline: Scott calls for high ARC standards, laments missed 'howler' in loss
news.body: Geelong's coach takes aim at the AFL's controversial review technology after yet another blunder cost his side dearly in the loss to Carlton.

news.headline: Pies eye ways to counter superstar Dog in crucial clash
news.body: After a stellar performance against Melbourne Marcus Bontempelli may be given a tag against Collingwood, says Craig McRae.
news.ai_summary: Collingwood's Harry Perryman looms as a potential tagger on Western Bulldogs star Marcus Bontempelli, having previously shut down Nasiah Wanganeen-Milera in Opening Round — a fantasy red flag for Bontempelli owners. The Magpies are without Darcy Moore (hamstring), Jamie Elliott (knee) and William Hayes (shoulder), with Tim Membrey, Wil Parker, Harvey Harrison and Angus Anderson coming in. Tim English returns from concussion for the Bulldogs, replacing Luke Cleary, restoring ruck value, while Cody Weightman's comeback is delayed until next week's Hawthorn clash.

news.headline: 'A lot of truth told': Lions get brutally honest after being belted by Giants
news.body: Chris Fagan says Brisbane players and staff sat down and talked about getting back to core values after savage loss.
news.ai_summary: Brisbane regains Jarrod Berry (calf) and Noah Answerth (concussion), with Cody Curtin earning a call-up after kicking four in the VFL, while Sam Marshall, James Tunstill and Will McLachlan were axed following the 78-point loss to GWS. Fremantle welcomes back skipper Alex Pearce (knee), but fantasy-relevant mids Hayden Young (concussion, third week out) and Caleb Serong (calf) remain sidelined — a major hit to Serong owners. The Lions' recent form slump (6-5) and back-to-back heavy losses could pressure scoring output from key Brisbane mids, while Fremantle rolls in on a club-record 10-game winning streak.

news.headline: Bombers assistant, club psychologist quit in wake of Scott sacking
news.body: A week of upheaval has continued at Essendon ahead of the Bombers' clash with West Coast.
news.ai_summary: Essendon's coaching upheaval continues with midfield assistant Ben Jacobs and club psychologist Ben Robbins departing Friday, days after senior coach Brad Scott was sacked. Brent Stanton takes over midfield assistant duties for Sunday's clash with West Coast at Optus Stadium — a role shift fantasy coaches should monitor for any Bombers midfield rotation/role changes. Scott exited after winning just one of his last 24 games, including a 17-game losing streak, with Ken Hinkley, Adam Simpson and James Hird floated as successors. No direct player price or break-even implications, but expect role volatility for Essendon mids until a new senior coach is appointed.

news.headline: Forward to miss months in a Giants double blow
news.body: Surgery has sidelined Jake Riccardi, the forward joining Jack Buckley in the medical room.
news.ai_summary: Avoid/downgrade Jake Riccardi (GWS), who will miss at least two months after ankle syndesmosis surgery — a clear sell for fantasy coaches. news.headline: From larrikin to leader, veteran finds new purpose in Giants forward line
news.body: Jake Stringer has become a steady hand for GWS as he helps shape the club's young forwards.
news.ai_summary: Jake Stringer (GWS) is enjoying a durable, high-output season after a hamstring-plagued 2025, kicking 23 goals in 11 games — already nearing last year's 25 from 15 matches — including a five-goal haul against Brisbane. With Aaron Cadman, Jesse Hogan and Jake Riccardi all sidelined at various points, Stringer has been the only consistent forward, boosting his fantasy ceiling as the focal target. Cadman's return and the breakout of Phoenix Gothard are worth monitoring as forward options, with Gothard a potential cash-cow watch. Stringer himself admits his goalkicking accuracy has been inconsistent, suggesting further scoring upside in the second half of the year.

Other names worth watching for role/form: Murphy Reid (Fremantle), Lawson Humphries (Geelong), Finn O'Sullivan (North Melbourne, now drawing tags after shutting down Zak Butters and Zach Merrett), Aliir Aliir (Port Adelaide) and Clayton Oliver (facing Melbourne).

news.headline: Hawks to seek clarity from League on contentious Watson non-goal
news.body: Sam Mitchell says Hawthorn will speak to the AFL after Nick Watson's goal on the half-time siren was disallowed for running off his line.
news.ai_summary: Hawthorn's Jack Gunston was benched for the entire final quarter as a precaution with a lingering foot injury, making him a risky hold despite his two early goals. Will Day got through his managed minutes cleanly, while Conor Nash (neck spasm) was a late withdrawal but should return next week vs the Western Bulldogs. St Kilda's Sam Flanders appears headed for a lengthy stint after being helped off injured, but coaches can pencil in Nasiah Wanganeen-Milera, Mitch Owens and Liam Ryan (all calf) to return next week against Sydney. Nick Watson's disallowed half-time goal doesn't change his output, but Hawthorn forwards including Blake Hardwick and Jack Ginnivan cashed in during the 52-point win.

news.headline: Sluggish Saints left scrambling as Hawks cash in on blistering start
news.body: Hawthorn records a 52-point win after keeping St Kilda goalless in the first half.
news.ai_summary: Sam Flanders (St Kilda) looks set for an extended stint on the sidelines after an awkward landing did serious damage to his Achilles, a major hit for owners. Returning Hawthorn veteran Jack Gunston (5 goals) reasserted his forward value, leapfrogging Ben King atop the Coleman, while Blake Hardwick (4 goals) again cashed in pushed forward. Will Day returned via the bench for Hawthorn finishing with 18 disposals and 5 clearances, and Nick Watson chipped in 21 and 2. For the Saints, Jack Silvagni (3 goals, 10 marks) impressed swung forward, while Tom De Koning, Alix Tauru and Rowan Marshall offered late marking targets in the 119-67 loss.

The 191cm Coburg forward leads the VFL goalkicking with 27 goals from eight games (plus four in the Gather Round state game) after finishing second in the Frosty Miller Medal last year with 54 goals. Converted from defender to forward late in 2023 by coach Jamie Cassidy-McNamara, Podhajski is a deep rookie-list watch only — any AFL debut would likely come as a forward-line injury cover, making him a low-priority cash-cow prospect at best.

news.headline: The 25: FIVE Dockers make our list of the best 25 players in the AFL
news.body: Welcome back to 'The 25', where AFL.com.au's team of experts rank the best players in the game.
news.ai_summary:
**Fantasy Implications from The 25:**

Tom Bolton is in career-best form (24.4 disposals, 4.4 clearances, 13 goals) as a midfield/forward and remains a strong POD option. Josh Treacy's career-high averages (13.8 disposals, 7.2 marks, 2.3 goals) make him a premium forward hold. Jy Simpkin... err, Jai Newcombe is producing career-best numbers (25.5 disposals, 7.6 clearances, 4.1 tackles) and is a must-have mid. Brodie Grundy (35.8 hitouts) is the clear #1 ruck, while Matt Rowell owners should monitor closely given injury-affected form, and Lachie Neale (30.7 disposals) is back to elite output.

news.headline: Bombers land Tassie speedster at No.1, rucks galore, Pies target experience

news.ai_summary: Essendon selected rebounding half-back Jaxon Artemis with pick No.1 in the Mid-Season Rookie Draft and, with 14 players (including Sam Durham via concussion) ruled out for the West Coast trip, the 19-year-old looms as an immediate rookie-cash option under caretaker coach Dean Solomon. Richmond grabbed defender Kye Annand at No.2 to cover Josh Gibcus, while West Coast took inside midfielder Oliver Francou (North Adelaide) at No.3 — a potential SuperCoach cash-cow given his clearance work. Ruck-needy coaches should note Carlton's Flynn Riley (No.4), Collingwood's Harrison Coe (No.8, covering Oscar Steene), Western Bulldogs' Caleb May (No.9) and Port Adelaide's Alex Van Wyk (No.14) all landing on lists. Collingwood also added mature-agers Liam Puncher (No.15) and 27-year-old Mitch Podhajski (No.18) for potential quick senior exposure.

news.headline: Bombers learn severity of injuries after brutal Dreamtime outing
news.body: The Bombers have confirmed the severity of several of the injuries they suffered on Friday night.
news.ai_summary: Trade out alert: Andrew McGrath (fractured jaw) and Matt Guelfi (hamstring tear) are both out until after Essendon's Round 15 bye, making them immediate trade targets before price drops hit. Sam Durham is in concussion protocols and will miss at least Round 12 vs West Coast — hold if you can stomach one week, but monitor closely. Jye Caldwell is under an injury cloud with an ankle/syndesmosis issue, adding further risk to Bomber owners. Archie May has been cleared and should play, offering some rare good news for cheapie holders.

news.headline: The five-hour drive that started Sun's long road to an AFL spot
news.body: Oscar Adams is establishing himself as a regular for Gold Coast's best 23 but it wouldn't have happened without his mum's long hours behind the wheel.
news.ai_summary: Oscar Adams has cemented himself in Gold Coast's best 23, playing seven games in 2026 as a key defender alongside Sam Collins and Mac Andrew, taking the spot vacated by the dropped Charlie Ballard. His role as an intercepting "come-forward defender" is locked in for now, with Ballard out of favour reducing job-security risk. Coming off arguably his best game against Port in Darwin, he's a deep-league DPP defender option for keeper leagues rather than a short-term cash cow, given his low base price and improving output. Monitor his scoring trend before pulling the trigger in Fantasy/SuperCoach.

news.headline: Hawks star makes return as AFL comeback draws nearer
news.body: Will Day makes his return from injury in the VFL on Friday night.
news.ai_summary: Will Day is unlikely to be an immediate selection option but his AFL return is edging closer after a managed VFL comeback for Box Hill (16 disposals, 6 tackles, 2 clearances) on Friday night. Coach Sam Mitchell confirmed Day will return when medically cleared, with no pressure on performance, suggesting at least another VFL hit-out before senior recall. With only six games in 2025 and a horror injury run (foot, shoulder), he's a risky POD but his midfield role and scoring ceiling make him one to monitor for trade-in once job security is confirmed.
